message.content:
Below is the complete list of **all tests** in the OWASP WSTG categories **WSTG-ATHZ** and **WSTG-SESS**, using the stable OWASP WSTG naming/numbering shown in the OWASP Foundation guide and the OWASP stable site.[4][6][7]

## WSTG-ATHZ — Authorization Testing

| WSTG ID | Test name | What it verifies | Canonical OWASP URL |
|---|---|---|---|
| WSTG-ATHZ-01 | **Testing Directory Traversal File Include** | Verifies whether the application improperly allows path traversal or file inclusion, enabling access to unauthorized files or server-side file inclusion behavior.[5] | https://owasp.org/www-project-web-security-testing-guide/latest/4-Web_Application_Security_Testing/05-Authorization_Testing/01-Testing_Directory_Traversal_File_Include |
| WSTG-ATHZ-02 | **Testing for Bypassing Authorization Schema** | Verifies whether authorization controls can be bypassed so a user can reach functions or resources they are not permitted to access.[1][4] | https://owasp.org/www-project-web-security-testing-guide/latest/4-Web_Application_Security_Testing/05-Authorization_Testing/02-Testing_for_Bypassing_Authorization_Schema |
| WSTG-ATHZ-03 | **Testing for Privilege Escalation** | Verifies whether a user can obtain privileges above their intended role, including horizontal or vertical escalation paths.[1][4] | https://owasp.org/www-project-web-security-testing-guide/latest/4-Web_Application_Security_Testing/05-Authorization_Testing/03-Testing_for_Privilege_Escalation |
| WSTG-ATHZ-04 | **Testing for Insecure Direct Object References** | Verifies whether direct references to objects such as records, files, or identifiers can be manipulated to access unauthorized data or actions.[1][4] | https://owasp.org/www-project-web-security-testing-guide/latest/4-Web_Application_Security_Testing/05-Authorization_Testing/04-Testing_for_Insecure_Direct_Object_References |
| WSTG-ATHZ-05 | **Testing for OAuth Weaknesses** | Verifies whether OAuth implementations are vulnerable to weaknesses in flow handling, token handling, configuration, or trust assumptions that could permit unauthorized access.[1][4] | https://owasp.org/www-project-web-security-testing-guide/latest/4-Web_Application_Security_Testing/05-Authorization_Testing/05-Testing_for_OAuth_Weaknesses |

## WSTG-SESS — Session Management Testing

| WSTG ID | Test name | What it verifies | Canonical OWASP URL |
|---|---|---|---|
| WSTG-SESS-01 | **Testing for Session Management Schema** | Verifies whether the application’s session management design is sound, including how sessions are created, handled, and associated with users.[1][4] | https://owasp.org/www-project-web-security-testing-guide/latest/4-Web_Application_Security_Testing/06-Session_Management_Testing/01-Testing_for_Session_Management_Schema |
| WSTG-SESS-02 | **Testing for Cookies Attributes** | Verifies whether session cookies use appropriate security attributes such as Secure, HttpOnly, and SameSite where applicable.[1][4] | https://owasp.org/www-project-web-security-testing-guide/latest/4-Web_Application_Security_Testing/06-Session_Management_Testing/02-Testing_for_Cookies_Attributes |
| WSTG-SESS-03 | **Testing for Session Fixation** | Verifies whether an attacker can force or preserve a known session identifier before authentication and then reuse it after login.[1][4] | https://owasp.org/www-project-web-security-testing-guide/latest/4-Web_Application_Security_Testing/06-Session_Management_Testing/03-Testing_for_Session_Fixation |
| WSTG-SESS-04 | **Testing for Exposed Session Variables** | Verifies whether session-related variables or identifiers are exposed through URLs, logs, client-side storage, or other unintended channels.[1][4] | https://owasp.org/www-project-web-security-testing-guide/latest/4-Web_Application_Security_Testing/06-Session_Management_Testing/04-Testing_for_Exposed_Session_Variables |
| WSTG-SESS-05 | **Testing for Cross Site Request Forgery** | Verifies whether the application accepts state-changing requests without adequate CSRF defenses tied to a user’s authenticated session.[1][4] | https://owasp.org/www-project-web-security-testing-guide/latest/4-Web_Application_Security_Testing/06-Session_Management_Testing/05-Testing_for_Cross_Site_Request_Forgery |
| WSTG-SESS-06 | **Testing for Logout Functionality** | Verifies whether logout properly invalidates the session and prevents continued use of the same token after sign-out.[1][4] | https://owasp.org/www-project-web-security-testing-guide/latest/4-Web_Application_Security_Testing/06-Session_Management_Testing/06-Testing_for_Logout_Functionality |
| WSTG-SESS-07 | **Testing Session Timeout** | Verifies whether inactive sessions expire appropriately and whether timeout behavior is enforced consistently.[1][4] | https://owasp.org/www-project-web-security-testing-guide/latest/4-Web_Application_Security_Testing/06-Session_Management_Testing/07-Testing_Session_Timeout |
| WSTG-SESS-08 | **Testing for Session Puzzling** | Verifies whether the application improperly reuses or combines session state in a way that lets attackers influence business flows or session logic.[1][4] | https://owasp.org/www-project-web-security-testing-guide/latest/4-Web_Application_Security_Testing/06-Session_Management_Testing/08-Testing_for_Session_Puzzling |
| WSTG-SESS-09 | **Testing for Session Hijacking** | Verifies whether session identifiers can be stolen or replayed to impersonate an authenticated user.[1][4] | https://owasp.org/www-project-web-security-testing-guide/latest/4-Web_Application_Security_Testing/06-Session_Management_Testing/09-Testing_for_Session_Hijacking |
| WSTG-SESS-10 | **Testing JSON Web Tokens** | Verifies whether JWT-based session or authentication tokens are implemented securely, including signing, validation, and claim handling.[1][4] | https://owasp.org/www-project-web-security-testing-guide/latest/4-Web_Application_Security_Testing/06-Session_Management_Testing/10-Testing_JSON_Web_Tokens |
| WSTG-SESS-11 | **Testing for Concurrent Sessions** | Verifies whether multiple simultaneous sessions are allowed or restricted according to the application’s security requirements, and whether concurrent-session handling is safe.[1][4] | https://owasp.org/www-project-web-security-testing-guide/latest/4-Web_Application_Security_Testing/06-Session_Management_Testing/11-Testing_for_Concurrent_Sessions |

If you want, I can also provide the same content in a CSV-like format or add the exact OWASP section titles for each test page.
